content_en: Love for the people what you love for yourself and you will be a Muslim. Do not laugh much for laughing too much deadens the heart." Recorded by al-Tirmidhi and Ahmad. Al-Amaoot seems to accept his hadith. (See his footnotes to ibn Rajah, Jaami, vol. 1, p. 252.) Saleem al-Hilaali explicitly states that due to its supporting narrations, it is a confirmed hadith. See al-Hilaali, Eeqaadh, p. 147. Hadith #9: "What I have Forbidden You, Avoid ... " more important to avoid the forbidden acts then to go beyond those obligatory acts into the non-obligatory acts. Indeed, avoiding the forbidden is obligatory while the recommended acts are supererogatory. wrote, Commenting on this aspect, al-Bugha and Mistu, two modem authors, From this point, one can see the mistake that many Muslims commit, especially during these times. This spreads contradiction in their lives. One finds a person very keen on doing the acts of obedience and the obligatory acts. He might even be very strict in following the recommended and beloved acts. However, one also finds him, at the same time, lax when it comes to doing forbidden acts. He might practice many forbidden acts. [For example,] one can find a fasting person taking part in interest transactions. A woman who made the Hajj and pays zakah leaves her house exposing her beauty [not wearing the proper hijaab ] ... Such people think that their act of worship will save them in Allah's sight... However, this goes against what Allah, the All-Wise, the Lawgiver has stated. It is confirmed in the sunnah of the leader of the messengers and the understanding of the leading noble Companions and Imams that the foundation of worship is to avoid what Allah has prohibited. [And,] the path of success is to strive against one's self and desires and make them avoid the forbidden acts ... Abdullah ibn Umar, the Imam of the worshippers, stated, "To return one-sixth of a dirham [illegally gotten] is preferred to spending one hundred thousand [dirhams] in the way of Allah." Al-Hasan al-Basri, the leader of the Followers, said, "The worshippers of Allah do not worship in any way better than avoiding what Allah has forbidden." Umar ibn Abdul Aziz also once said, "Taqwa is not praying during the late night and fasting during the day and mixing [between permissible and impermissible] between those two. But Taqwa is to fulfill what Allah has obligated and avoid what Allah has forbidden. If, in addition to that, there are some [good voluntary] deeds, it is goodness upon goodness." This shows us that avoiding the forbidden takes precedence over doing the acts of obedience. This does not mean, as we said, that a Muslim should be lax in performing the obligatory deeds. This is how some of those people who have diseased hearts and weak souls behave. They are very lax and lazy when it comes to Allah's law and they do not do what Allah has obligated. Then they claim that they are better than those who pray and fast due to the fact that their behavior with the people is good and the religion means to have good dealings with the Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi people... These people, like those described above, have left the path of guidance ... 1 Hence, both approaches are wrong. A Muslim must perform the acts that are obligatory upon him while at the same time completely remaining away from the forbidden acts. Al-Baitaar, another modem author, echoed their views when he wrote, We see some people very keen on using the miswaak (the toothstick) while they do not avoid backbiting, spreading tales and having forbidden wealth. They will argue about covering the head if they are behind an Imam whose head is uncovered while they will not be careful about lying in their dealings or looking at what Allah has forbidden one to look at. [People must be aware of how incorrect this situation is.]2 "Verily, the people before you were destroyed only because of their excessive questioning" As was clearly demonstrated in the Hadith of Jibreel, not all questioning is bad. The Angel Jibreel came and asked the Prophet (peace be upon him) important and beneficial questions. Indeed, there are some questions that are considered fardh ain or an obligation upon the individual. Those are the questions that one needs to ask in order to understand and practice the religion correctly, such as how to pray, how to fast and so forth. Therefore, to understand this portion of the hadith, one has to understand what types of questions are being referred to. The same is true for the verse in the Quran in which Allah says, J ,,.. ,,.. 0 6J ,,. 0қ Û'; ½ ¿ 0 ;T: \i :; 1_,J•੭j- 1࢈1; J:UI ,- .!i ,,.. ,., J ,.. .,. ,., ࢇ j hlj ࢆ ࢉ1 ऍ Ҝ ࢅ S1;ࢊ1 0ࢋࢌࢍ1)•੍.'; "O believers! Ask not about things which, if made plain to you, may cause you trouble.
content_id:
Lebih penting menghindari perbuatan terlarang daripada melampaui amalan wajib ke amalan-amalan sunnah. Sesungguhnya menghindari yang haram adalah wajib, sementara amalan-amalan yang dianjurkan bersifat sunnah.

Al-Bugha dan Mistu, dua penulis kontemporer, menulis: Dari titik ini kita dapat melihat kesalahan yang banyak dilakukan kaum Muslim, terutama di zaman ini. Ini menyebarkan kontradiksi dalam kehidupan mereka. Seseorang sangat antusias dalam melakukan ketaatan dan amalan wajib, bahkan sangat ketat dalam mengikuti amalan sunnah. Namun pada saat yang sama ia lalai dalam menghindari perbuatan haram dan mempraktikkan banyak di antaranya. Misalnya, dapat ditemukan orang yang berpuasa namun terlibat dalam transaksi riba. Seorang wanita yang telah berhaji dan membayar zakat keluar rumah dengan memperlihatkan kecantikannya tanpa hijab yang benar. Orang-orang seperti ini mengira bahwa ibadah mereka akan menyelamatkan mereka di sisi Allah. Namun hal ini bertentangan dengan apa yang ditetapkan Allah Yang Mahabijaksana. Telah dipastikan dalam sunnah bahwa fondasi ibadah adalah menghindari apa yang Allah haramkan.

Abdullah ibn Umar menyatakan: 'Mengembalikan seperenam dirham yang diperoleh secara haram lebih utama daripada menginfakkan seratus ribu dirham di jalan Allah.' Al-Hasan al-Bashri berkata: 'Para hamba Allah tidak beribadah dengan cara yang lebih baik daripada menghindari apa yang Allah haramkan.' Umar ibn Abdul Aziz pernah berkata: 'Takwa bukanlah salat malam dan puasa di siang hari sambil mencampuradukkan (yang halal dan haram) di antara keduanya. Takwa adalah memenuhi apa yang Allah wajibkan dan menjauhi apa yang Allah haramkan. Jika selain itu ada beberapa amalan (sunnah), itu adalah kebaikan di atas kebaikan.'

Ini menunjukkan bahwa menghindari yang terlarang lebih didahulukan daripada melakukan amalan ketaatan. Ini bukan berarti seorang Muslim boleh lalai dalam melaksanakan amalan wajib. Al-Baitaar, penulis kontemporer lain, menulis: Kita melihat sebagian orang sangat antusias menggunakan siwak (kayu pembersih gigi) sementara mereka tidak menghindari ghibah, menyebarkan fitnah, dan memiliki harta haram. Mereka berdebat tentang menutup kepala jika berada di belakang imam yang kepalanya terbuka, sementara mereka tidak berhati-hati dalam berbohong dalam transaksi atau memandang apa yang Allah haramkan.

'Sesungguhnya orang-orang sebelum kalian binasa hanya karena banyak bertanya.' Tidak semua pertanyaan itu buruk, sebagaimana jelas ditunjukkan dalam Hadis Jibril. Memang ada pertanyaan yang dianggap fardhu ain — yaitu pertanyaan yang diperlukan untuk memahami dan mempraktikkan agama dengan benar, seperti bagaimana salat, bagaimana berpuasa, dan sebagainya. Allah berfirman: 'Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, janganlah kamu menanyakan hal-hal yang jika diterangkan kepadamu akan menyusahkan kamu.' Untuk memahami bagian hadis ini, seseorang harus memahami jenis pertanyaan apa yang dimaksud.
content_ar: وفي حديث موصى به للمسلمين: «أَحِبَّ لِلنَّاسِ مَا تُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِكَ تَكُنْ مُسْلِماً، وَلَا تَكْثُرُ الضَّحِكَ فَإِنَّ كَثْرَةَ الضَّحِكِ تُمِيتُ القَلْبَ» رواه الترمذي وأحمد، وهذا الحديث يجمع بين أصلين جليلين: الأول محبة الخير للمسلمين كمحبته للنفس، وهو تطبيق عملي لمبدأ النصيحة. والثاني التحذير من الإسراف في الضحك الذي يُميت القلب ويُفسد نور الإيمان. وكلاهما متعلق بحفظ الأخوة الإسلامية وصحة القلب وسلامته. والمسلم الكامل الإسلام هو من يريد لإخوانه من الخير ما يريده لنفسه ويكف عن كل ما يُذهب رقة القلب ونور الإيمان.
